lines: The ledger is written as lessons, not confession. Lesson one: a body that stays whole is only a locked chapel. Open it, and the door is always the same door.
Lesson two: feed the opened flesh of the saint to the closed-flesh living, and the living open faster. Cut slow. Let the saint grow back between meals.

A later hand keeps stock below the lessons: 'One on the cross. One held in the cellar for after.' Then a travel note, different ink: 'Hallowfen has stopped answering its signal bells. Carry witness before the fields are sealed.'
